reasoning: The Bank Term Funding Program (BTFP) was an emergency lending facility created in March 2023 and stopped making new loans on March 11, 2024. Because loans were made for up to one year, all outstanding loans matured by early March 2025. This is confirmed by the FRED series data, which hit exactly 0.0 on March 19, 2025, and has remained at 0.0 through early June 2025. For the weekly average to increase, the Federal Reserve would have to revive the BTFP under the same name and series within the next few months, which is extremely unlikely given that the original program legally expired and the Federal Reserve typically does not revive expired emergency facilities identically. Therefore, the probability of any increase from 0 is at the minimum possible bound. I slightly increased the probability for the most distant date to reflect generic long-horizon uncertainty as requested, though practically speaking, the facility is completely wound down and is overwhelmingly likely to stay at zero indefinitely.
